module_signals.finding: Evidence base is solid for a one-pass live run. FY2025 (reported 2026-01-28: net sales EUR32.7B, net income EUR9.6B, GM 52.8%, EPS basic EUR24.73, Q4 bookings EUR13.2B incl EUR7.4B EUV, YE backlog EUR38.8B, EUR7.50 dividend, EUR12B buyback) and Q1 2026 (reported 2026-04-15: net sales EUR8.8B, EUV systems EUR4.1B of EUR6.3B, service EUR2.5B, GM 53.0%, net income EUR2.8B, EPS EUR7.15, 79 systems, China 19% of system sales, Korea 45%, FY2026 guide RAISED to EUR36-40B) are corroborated across ASML's official releases + 6-K + IR deck. Price $1769.32 (last settled NASDAQ close 2026-07-02) confirmed by Yahoo + stockanalysis + WallStreetZen; shares ~385.4M (WSZ 385,417,665; AGM filing 388.15M issued - 3.22M treasury); mcap ~$681.9B (identity-consistent). OPEN ITEMS that cap completeness: (O1) FY2025 net income data conflict -- stockanalysis EUR10,213M/EPS EUR26.26 vs ASML-primary EUR9.6B/EUR24.73 (primary used, load-bearing); (O2) ASML STOPPED publishing quarterly net bookings from 2026 -> Q1'26 order intake not disclosed (backlog EUR38.8B is the last hard number); (O3) mcap source spread ($681.9B identity vs $719.4B stockanalysis on a higher share basis; identity used); (O4) no direct SEC 20-F full-text extraction this pass. Completeness ~65% -> caps at DECISION_DRAFT/STARTER-ceiling on completeness, though M6 PRICE caps below that.

ASML is the single deepest, most durable chokepoint in the semiconductor stack: the SOLE producer of EUV lithography worldwide -- the only tool able to pattern leading-edge logic (TSMC/Samsung/Intel N2/A16) and advanced DRAM/HBM. THE MECHANISM: the AI-capex buildout (GPUs/TPUs/Trainium) pulls leading-edge + HBM wafers, which are EUV-intensive; every advanced AI chip is therefore printed on a machine only ASML can make -- a literal 'toll on every advanced chip,' upstream of NVDA / Google TPU / AWS Trainium. Q1 2026 evidences the mechanism working THROUGH the China cliff: China fell 36%->19% of system sales but gross margin HELD at 53.0% because lost low-margin DUV was replaced by high-margin AI-driven EUV (Korea became #1 at 45%, memory for AI). High-NA EUV (0.55 NA, EXE:5200) extends the moat a generation (Intel deployed first, Dec 2025; Samsung/SK hynix 2027-28). ASML's own 2030 model: EUR44-60B sales, 56-60% GM, double-digit EUV CAGR. Business +2. PORTFOLIO NOTE (must flag): ASML is an AI-STACK name -- it ADDS to the user's BTC/GOOGL/NBIS 'AI+liquidity' single factor; it is NOT a diversifier. BUT it is a uniquely differentiated chokepoint: a 100% monopoly toll one layer deeper than owning another GPU/cloud name.

Profit pool is EXCEPTIONAL and the moat is the deepest in tech. EUV is a 100% monopoly -- not 'leading share' but ZERO qualified competitors -- built on 30+ years of R&D, the owned Cymer light source, the Zeiss high-NA optics ecosystem (ASML holds a stake), and impossible customer switching costs. This is a STRONGER moat than HWM's airfoils (HWM has peers + captive OEM capacity; ASML has none). Gross margin ~50-53% (world-class for capital equipment) trending to a 56-60% target; installed-base service (~28% of sales) is a growing, decades-long annuity. OPERATOR: Christophe Fouquet (CEO since 2024-04-25) is a physicist (Grenoble INP MSc) who PERSONALLY industrialized EUV as EVP EUV (2018) after a 16-yr internal rise -- a hardship-formed technical builder, exactly the archetype this investor favors over political-courtier/pure-finance operators. Governance clean: two-tier board, strict one-share-one-vote, no dual class; the Stichting preference-share option is a takeover DEFENSE, not entrenchment. Grade 4.5/5: elite domain fit + clean governance; docked only for short (~2yr) CEO tenure, buyback executed at ~62x/near-ATH, and dropped quarterly-bookings disclosure. Moat DEEP & DURABLE; the risk is to VOLUME (cyclical) and MULTIPLE, not to the monopoly position.

Financials CONFIRM an exceptional, high-margin, cash-generative monopoly -- AND prove it is a DEEP CYCLICAL. 5-yr trend: revenue EUR18,611M (2021) -> EUR32,667M (2025) [+12% CAGR]; gross margin steady ~50-53%; net income EUR5,883M -> EUR9,600M; FCF EUR9,945M (2021) -> EUR4,381M (2023 DOWN-CYCLE trough, -55%) -> EUR12,253M (2025). The 2023 FCF trough is the tell: this is a cyclical wearing a compounder's margin, so it MUST be valued off normalized, not peak, earnings. Q1 2026: net sales EUR8.8B, GM 53.0%, net income EUR2.8B (31.4% margin), EUR1.1B buyback. Balance sheet is a FORTRESS: net cash, EUR12B buyback + EUR7.50 dividend funded from FCF, no solvency risk. Capex light (~5% of sales); the heavy spend is offensive R&D (~EUR4B/yr). NORMALIZATION FLAGS: (a) value off through-cycle earnings (2023 trough vs 2025 peak); (b) China 33%->~20% structural step-down; (c) bookings disclosure dropped (2026); (d) FY2025 net-income data conflict (O1, primary EUR9.6B used); (e) EUR->USD FX in the multiple. Signal +2 for quality, read WITH the cyclicality flag that caps the size ceiling.

NO permanent-impairment / balance-sheet-death risk: 100% EUV monopoly + High-NA + service annuity + net cash -> hard veto does NOT fire. The traps are VALUATION, CYCLICALITY, CHINA/POLICY, and (book-specific) FACTOR CONCENTRATION -- not a business kill. KILL/DOWNSIDE PATHS: (F1) MULTIPLE COMPRESSION -- ~62x trailing / ~48x EV/peak-FCF (~68-75x on NORMALIZED FCF) de-rating toward a still-premium 25-30x is the dominant driver; (F2) AI-capex / semi-capex DIGESTION -- a cyclical air-pocket cuts revenue hard even with the monopoly intact (2023 FCF fell ~55%); (F3) CHINA EXPORT CONTROLS (LIVE, evolving) -- the MATCH Act (Apr 2026) would ban ALL DUV to China (~20% of 2026 rev) with a 150-day NL-alignment clock, and a June 2026 BIS/EUV-in-China allegation (ASML formally denies, 'all 314 machines accounted') creates component-enforcement/fine risk (cf. Applied Materials' Feb 2026 settlement); (F4) HIGH-NA disappoints -- TSMC already declined High-NA for 1.4nm on ~$400M/tool cost, risking the upper half of the 2030 model; (F5) customer concentration (~5 customers ARE the demand); (F6) indigenous EUV (SMEE) closing the ~10-15yr gap (low near-term, very high severity -- the one moat risk); (F7) FACTOR -- ASML ADDS to the book's BTC/GOOGL/NBIS AI-capex factor, so an AI-capex winter hits all four together. THE CORE TRAP: a 5y IRR that is NEGATIVE in the base case (~-5.7%/yr) even on ASML's OWN mid-Investor-Day 2030 targets + a 28x exit. Signal -1 (valuation/cycle/policy trap), not -2 (no business kill).

At $1769.32 (~$681.9B mkt cap, ~$676B EV): trailing P/E ~62x (FY2025 EPS EUR24.73 = $28.25), forward P/E ~43x (2026E EUR35.95 = $41.07; ~47x on stockanalysis' lower estimate), EV/FY2025 FCF ~48x, EV/NORMALIZED FCF ~68-75x. 52-wk context: -11.5% off ATH $1,999.96; +159% off 52-wk low $683.48 (a near-high entry after the stock roughly tripled -- not a low/high grab error, just very expensive on peak-cycle earnings). 5-YEAR IRR (to FY2030, ~4.5yr, incl ~0.48% div), anchored on ASML's OWN Investor Day 2030 model: BEAR (EUR40B rev, 28% net margin, de-rate to 24x, EPS ~$35) ~-14.8%/yr; BASE (EUR50B mid-model, 30% margin, 28x, EPS ~$48) ~-5.7%/yr; BULL (EUR60B top-model, 32% margin, 32x, EPS ~$62) ~+2.9%/yr. ALL THREE fail an 8% hurdle; base is NEGATIVE on the company's own optimistic midpoint. Buy-below for an 8% base IRR ~$960 (=~46% below today, still ~44x fwd = only 'fair' for a monopoly, not cheap); CORE (10% IRR, 26x exit) ~$821. ZERO margin of safety. Signal -2: price actively forbids new money -- the textbook 'best business, wrong price' verdict on PEAK-cycle earnings (the HWM/GEV pattern, more extreme). New money = WATCH; existing (N/A, not held) = HOLD the monopoly, do not add, do not chase above ~$1,900.

starter_zone: <= ~$960 (where a mid-Investor-Day 2030 rev path of EUR50B begins to clear an 8% 5y IRR on a still-premium 28x exit; pays for the EUV monopoly + service annuity, not for peak-cycle earnings at a permanent 62x). This is ~46% below today and still ~44x forward -- a monopoly only stops being an actively bad new-money bet here; it is not 'cheap.' It would take an AI-capex scare or a China shock to get there.
add_zone: First STARTER at <= ~$960; add toward Confirmed (cap ~11%, deep-cyclical + AI-factor-concentrated in THIS book -> below the 15-25% Core tier) only as: (a) AI-driven EUV demand + backlog keep compounding through the China cliff, (b) the MATCH Act / BIS-China risk resolves without a material permanent revenue hit, (c) High-NA adoption broadens beyond Intel. Fund a STARTER partly by trimming another AI-factor name, NOT by stacking AI exposure. No add at current price.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Do not chase above ~$1,900-2,000 (at/above the ATH; base-case 5y IRR worsens toward -8%/yr). Trim an existing position on any of: MATCH Act enacted with a permanent revenue hit, an AI-capex-winter order air-pocket, a China indigenous-EUV breakthrough, or a multiple pushed back to the ATH without an earnings step-up. High weight alone is NOT a trim reason -- only thesis/price/policy triggers.
binding_constraint: PRICE on NORMALIZED earnings -- not completeness, not business quality. ASML is the best business in this analysis: a 100% EUV monopoly, net cash, a growing service annuity, a technologist CEO, and a genuine toll on every AI chip. The binding constraint is that $1769.32 = ~62x trailing / ~48x EV/peak-FCF on PEAK-cycle earnings, so even the BASE 5-year IRR is negative (~-5.7%/yr) computed on ASML's OWN mid-Investor-Day 2030 targets plus a still-premium 28x exit. A secondary, book-level constraint: ASML ADDS to (does not diversify) the BTC/GOOGL/NBIS AI factor. Completeness (~65%) would cap at STARTER, but PRICE caps harder -> WATCH. Quintessential 'best possible business, unbuyable price' -- the HWM/GEV pattern, more extreme.
kill_criteria: K-A China export controls (TOP): the MATCH Act is enacted banning ALL DUV to China (~20% of 2026 rev) AND the loss is NOT offset by AI EUV, OR a BIS enforcement action/fine on the June 2026 EUV-component allegation, OR controls escalate toward EUV components -> re-underwrite the revenue base and the moat-adjacent policy risk. Status as of as_of: MATCH Act pending; BIS allegation open, ASML formally denies.

K-B AI-capex digestion: a semi-capex / AI-capex down-cycle cuts EUV order intake and revenue (the 2023 FCF-trough pattern) -> the cyclical-peak-multiple trap materializes -> avoid / reduce. Watch the backlog vs the EUR38.8B YE2025 anchor (harder to see now that quarterly bookings are undisclosed).
K-C Gross-margin reversal below ~50% (mix shift, pricing, or a demand air-pocket) -> the monopoly-pricing-power leg weakens -> downgrade.
K-D High-NA broadly deferred/rejected on cost (following TSMC's 1.4nm decision) -> the upper half of the EUR44-60B 2030 model is at risk -> cut the growth assumption.

K-E Indigenous EUV: China (SMEE) closes the ~10-15yr gap or demonstrates a viable EUV path -> the exclusivity of the monopoly erodes -> re-underwrite the durability premium (low near-term probability, highest severity).
K-F Multiple/IRR breach: price rises back toward the ATH ~$1,900-2,000 without a commensurate earnings step-up -> negative expected value even in the bull case -> trim/exit any position.
K-G Capital-allocation slip: buybacks accelerated at >70x earnings, or value-destructive M&A -> dock operator grade, reduce size ceiling.
runner_dissent: I have very high conviction in the BUSINESS -- higher than for HWM -- and want to flag what the mechanical -2 on M6 understates. (1) ASML is arguably the single best business in this entire book: a literal 100% monopoly on the deepest chokepoint in the most important supply chain of the era, with a service annuity, net cash, a physicist-builder CEO, and clean governance. If the AI-capex super-cycle has more and longer legs than a normalized model assumes -- and the record Q4'25 EUR13.2B bookings + raised 2026 guide + Korea/AI EUV replacing China DUV all argue it might -- then 'normalizing' the earnings understates the through-cycle level, and both my EPS path and my exit multiple are too conservative; the bull case may be low. (2) The moat is more durable than HWM's, so a permanent re-rating to a 30x+ monopoly multiple is more defensible here than for a cyclical airfoil maker. NET: the WATCH verdict is still correct -- there is genuinely zero margin of safety at $1,769 and the base IRR is negative even on ASML's own 2030 midpoint, and (crucially for THIS book) it does not diversify the AI factor. But this is the name I would MOST want to own at the right price, and a 30-45% pullback (toward ~$960-1,100, plausible on any AI-capex scare or China escalation) would flip it to STARTER fast. The risk of the WATCH call is missing the best chokepoint in tech because it simply never gets cheap; the discipline is to wait for price AND to size it aware it stacks, not diversifies, the book's single factor. One real uncertainty: my exit multiples (24x/28x/32x) are the swing factor -- if the market permanently capitalizes EUV-monopoly quality at 40x+, today's price is defensible and WATCH would be wrong on price (though the factor-concentration caution would remain).
